page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance type powered by the KVM hypervisor and built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with 64 vCPUs, utilizing Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps to a dedicated physical core, eliminating resource contention from hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core.

This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage. For network-intensive workloads, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput communication with other cloud resources and storage volumes. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation and balanced resource profile offer a predictable performance-per-resource ratio for enterprise workloads that require consistent CPU execution without the overhead of shared-core environments.

The Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.16xlarge is suited for multi-threaded applications, enterprise web serving, application servers, and medium-sized database deployments. The combination of 30 Gbps network bandwidth and dedicated Intel Xeon processing cores also makes it appropriate for distributed caching, backend services, and CI/CD pipelines that demand stable, non-burstable compute performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g5ne.16xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8163 processors running at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps to support high-throughput data transfers. This resource profile makes the instance highly suitable for enterprise application servers, web serving, distributed caching, and database workloads requiring predictable, non-burstable compute performance.
